_comment: MARCUS Cardiac MRI Q&A generation templates. Used by scripts/build_dataset.py.
generation_guidance: CMR studies are multi-sequence DICOM series. Available sequences: cine (short-axis stack, 2-chamber, 4-chamber, LVOT), LGE (late gadolinium enhancement), T1 mapping (pre- and post-contrast), T2 mapping, perfusion (stress/rest). Questions must specify which sequence and view they relate to. Focus on: LV/RV function from cine (biventricular volumes, EF, wall motion), myocardial scar/fibrosis patterns on LGE (ischaemic subendocardial vs. non-ischaemic mid-wall/epicardial), T1/T2 elevation (oedema, inflammation, amyloid), perfusion defects (fixed vs. reversible), and cardiomyopathy phenotypes (HCM with SAM/LVOTO, DCM, ARVC with RV dysplasia, cardiac sarcoidosis, cardiac amyloidosis, myocarditis).

example_questions.question: Based on the LGE images, what pattern of myocardial enhancement is present?
mcq_example: Subendocardial enhancement in an ischaemic distribution (LAD territory)
Mid-wall linear enhancement consistent with non-ischaemic cardiomyopathy

example_questions.question: What is the left ventricular ejection fraction estimated from this short-axis cine stack?
What does the elevated native T1 and T2 signal in this study most suggest?
Which cardiomyopathy diagnosis is best supported by the CMR findings in this study?
mcq_example: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) with asymmetric septal hypertrophy

sequence_selection_guidance: The metadata-driven sequence selector in MARCUS uses DICOM sequence description tags to automatically select the most relevant sequences for a given question. Ensure question templates specify which sequence they relate to (cine, LGE, T1 map, T2 map, perfusion) so the selector can route correctly.
pretrain_focus: Structured CMR report descriptions covering biventricular function, LGE findings, tissue characterisation results, and final impression — mirroring a standard CMR report.
sft_focus: Diverse Q&A spanning all CMR sequences and cardiomyopathy diagnoses. Include questions that require integrating findings across multiple sequences.
grpo_focus: Unambiguous MCQ questions tied to specific sequence findings. Correct answer should be visually verifiable from the specified CMR sequence. Distractors should differ in CMR appearance.
